title: justicia leptantha subsp. late-ovata
intro: Justicia leptantha subsp. late-ovata, also known as Late-ovate Water Willow, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae. It is a perennial herb that has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and supporting digestion, making it a popular choice in herbal remedies. Late-ovate Water Willow can be prepared as a tea or tincture, and is often used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
